name: Atmus Filtration Technologies Inc.
business: Atmus Filtration Technologies designs and manufactures advanced filtration products, primarily under the Fleetguard brand, for on-highway commercial vehicles and off-highway agriculture, construction, mining, and power generation equipment. The company generates approximately 86% of its net sales from aftermarket replacement parts and 14% from first-fit sales to original equipment manufacturers (OEMs). Key customers include Cummins, PACCAR, and the Traton Group, which together accounted for a significant portion of 2025 net sales.
sellsTo: off-highway equipment OEMs (agriculture, construction, mining, power generation)
aftermarket channels (OEM dealers, independent distributors, retail outlets)

exposures.sentence: Specifically, our core markets may be impacted by technology transitions, including the transition to battery-electric vehicles, hydrogen-powered internal combustion engines, fuel cell electric vehicles and alternate power sources.
Expand into Industrial Filtration Markets: Build sustainable growth by expanding and diversifying into the industrial filtration market.

Atmus technical team develops a range of filtration technologies, including filtration media, filter element formation, filtration systems integration and service-related solutions such as remote digital diagnostic and prognostic platforms and analytics.
Substantially all of our net sales are related to internal combustion engine filtration products.

Concerns regarding the effects of emissions of GHG on the climate have driven (and will likely continue to drive) international, national, regional and local legislative and regulatory responses, including those imposing more stringent emissions standards, requiring higher fuel efficiency and/or banning sales of gas-powered vehicles in the future.
Atmus is one of the global leaders of filtration products for on-highway commercial vehicles and off-highway agriculture, construction, mining and power generation vehicles and equipment.

catalysts.event: Acquisition of a business in the industrial filtration market
catalysts.sentence: For instance, in 2026 we closed the acquisition of a business in the industrial filtration market

catalysts.event: Entry into Amended and Restated Credit Agreement
catalysts.sentence: Subsequent to current year end, on January 7, 2026, the Company entered into an Amended and Restated Credit Agreement which provided for a term loan facility of $1.0 billion and $500 million revolving credit facility, both of which mature on January 7, 2031.

catalysts.event: Transition services agreement with Cummins ends
catalysts.sentence: The transition services agreement under which Cummins had continued to provide certain services related primarily to administrative services ended in September 2025.
